title: Understanding, experience and attitudes towards artificial intelligence technologies for clinical decision support in hearing health: a mixed-methods survey of healthcare professionals in the UK
justification: The paper discusses healthcare professionals' attitudes towards AI technologies in clinical decision support within the context of hearing health, which relates to the use and understanding of knowledge and digital tools in healthcare practice.

title: Knowledge, attitudes, and concerns about psilocybin and MDMA as novel therapies among U.S. healthcare professionals
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' knowledge and attitudes towards novel therapies, specifically psychedelic-assisted therapy, which relates to the use and understanding of evidence-based practice in a healthcare context.

title: Healthcare professionals’ perception of using a web-based reminiscence therapy to support person with dementia during the COVID-19 pandemic
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' perceptions of a digital tool (web-based reminiscence therapy) used in dementia care, aligning with the inclusion criteria related to the use of knowledge and information systems in healthcare contexts.

title: Education and clinical decision support for healthcare professionals on emergency preparedness for extremely dangerous pathogens: report of a conference workshop
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' education and clinical decision support related to emergency preparedness for infectious diseases, aligning with the inclusion criteria regarding professional healthcare practice and the application of knowledge in healthcare contexts.

title: Instruments for assessing healthcare professionals' knowledge and skills of evidence-based practice: A scoping review.
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' knowledge and skills related to evidence-based practice, which aligns with the inclusion criteria regarding the use and understanding of research evidence in healthcare contexts.

title: What do healthcare professionals want from a resource to support person-centred conversations on physical activity? A mixed-methods, user-centric approach to developing educational resources
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' needs for resources to support person-centred conversations about physical activity, aligning with the inclusion criteria related to professional healthcare practice and the use of knowledge in clinical contexts.

title: Exploration of the views and experiences of research healthcare professionals during their redeployment to clinical roles during the COVID‐19 pandemic
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' experiences and adaptation during redeployment to clinical roles, which relates to professional practice and the use of knowledge in healthcare contexts.

title: ImpENSA eHealthy Conversation Skills training for healthcare professionals aimed at improving micronutrient status during the first 1000 days in South Africa
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' training and knowledge application related to nutrition and behaviour change support, which aligns with the inclusion criteria regarding healthcare practice and the use of knowledge in professional contexts.

title: Perspectives of healthcare professionals on the use of immersive virtual reality in teenage and young adult oncology: a qualitative interview study
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' perspectives on the use of immersive virtual reality in oncology, relating to the application of digital tools in a healthcare context, which aligns with the inclusion criteria.

title: Dietary intervention in gestational diabetes: a qualitative study of the acceptability and feasibility of a novel whole-diet intervention in healthcare professionals
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' views on a dietary intervention for gestational diabetes, relating to the use and acceptability of a novel intervention in a healthcare context, which aligns with the inclusion criteria.

title: Bioethical knowledge in students and health professionals: a systematic review
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' understanding and application of bioethical knowledge, which is relevant to professional healthcare practice and education. It discusses the importance of training in bioethics, aligning with the inclusion criteria related to knowledge-related processes in healthcare.

title: Towards a Knowledge Graph-Based Explainable Decision Support System in Healthcare
justification: The paper discusses a knowledge graph-based framework for AI-based clinical decision support systems, which relates to healthcare professionals' use of digital tools and decision-support technologies in healthcare contexts.

title: Unmet needs and gaps in the identification of secondary progression in multiple sclerosis: a Southern Italy healthcare professionals’ perspective
justification: The paper discusses the identification and management of multiple sclerosis from the perspective of healthcare professionals, focusing on the use of knowledge and digital tools in healthcare contexts, which aligns with the inclusion criteria.

title: The Medical Science Liaison Role in Spain: A Survey About the Opinion of HealthCare Professionals
justification: The paper concerns healthcare professionals' understanding and opinion of the Medical Science Liaison role, which relates to the use and application of knowledge in professional healthcare contexts.

title: A pilot exploration of staff and service-user perceptions of a novel digital health technology (Virtual Engagement Rehabilitation Assistant) in complex inpatient rehabilitation
justification: The paper discusses a digital health technology in the context of rehabilitation, focusing on healthcare professionals' and service-users' perceptions, which aligns with the inclusion criteria related to healthcare practice and the use of digital tools.

title: How Commercially Available Virtual Reality-Based Interventions Are Delivered and Reported in Gait, Posture, and Balance Rehabilitation: A Systematic Review.
justification: The paper concerns healthcare and rehabilitation, specifically focusing on the use of virtual reality technologies in physical rehabilitation, which relates to healthcare professionals' roles and practices. It discusses the implementation of interventions and the involvement of therapists, aligning with the inclusion criteria.

title: Advanced Technology in a Real-World Rehabilitation Setting: Longitudinal Observational Study on Clinician Adoption and Implementation
justification: The paper discusses the implementation and adoption of advanced technology in a rehabilitation setting, focusing on healthcare professionals' use and management of these technologies, which aligns with the inclusion criteria related to healthcare practice and evidence-based approaches.

title: AB1354-HPR CURRENT SITUATION OF TRAINING IN RHEUMATOLOGY IN THE POSTGRADUATE CURRICULUMS OF INTERNAL MEDICINE, FAMILY MEDICINE, GERIATRICS, PAIN MEDICINE AND, PHYSICAL AND REHABILITATION MEDICINE ACROSS COLOMBIA
justification: The paper discusses the training status in rheumatology within various postgraduate medical specialties in Colombia, which relates to professional healthcare practice and education. It also addresses the use of digital resources in the training process, aligning with the inclusion criteria regarding healthcare professionals' use of knowledge and information systems.

title: Clinnova Federated Learning Proof of Concept: Key Takeaways from a Cross-border Collaboration
justification: The paper discusses the use of artificial intelligence and data science in enhancing healthcare outcomes, specifically in the context of a collaborative initiative aimed at improving patient care for multiple sclerosis, which aligns with the inclusion criteria related to healthcare professionals and digital health technologies.

title: Medical Data in the Digital Era - Legal Challenges Related to Providing Information Security, Applying GDPR and Respecting the Professional Secrecy
justification: The paper discusses the challenges of processing medical data in electronic form, focusing on information security and legal regulations in healthcare, which aligns with the inclusion criteria related to healthcare professionals' management and application of information systems.

title: An overview of Personalized Medicine landscape and policies in the European Union
justification: The paper discusses healthcare policies related to Personalized Medicine in the European Union, which aligns with healthcare research and the management of knowledge in professional healthcare contexts.

title: The challenge of bringing evidence in decision-making for public health emergencies: can preparedness reach out to HTA?
justification: The paper discusses evidence-based decision-making in public health, focusing on the use of digital health tools and Health Technology Assessment (HTA) in healthcare contexts, which aligns with the inclusion criteria related to healthcare professionals' use of knowledge and information systems.

title: Constitutional foundations of cross-border digital healthcare
justification: The paper discusses legal regulation and constitutional aspects of digital healthcare, which relates to healthcare professionals' use and management of information systems in healthcare contexts. It also addresses the integration of law and biomedicine, relevant to professional healthcare practice.

title: Implementing a Cross-Border Next-Generation Personal Health Record in the Philippines and Taiwan: An Implementation Case Report Using Health Level 7 International Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources
justification: The paper discusses the implementation of a cross-border personal health record system, which relates to healthcare professionals' use of information systems and addresses interoperability in healthcare contexts.

title: Interoperability Challenges and Critical Success Factors in the Deployment of Cross-border Digital Medical Prescriptions in Finland and Estonia
justification: The paper discusses interoperability challenges and critical success factors related to digital medical prescriptions, which pertains to healthcare systems and the application of digital tools in professional healthcare contexts.
